items.title: Hornets updated salary cap situation after re-signing Coby White.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Hornets updated salary cap situation after re-signing Coby White.

Here's a potential structure if they start high and descend it:

2026-27: $26.8M
2027-28: $24.7M
2028-29: $22.5M

I’ll use this projection based on the Hornets’ recent track record of flat/descending contracts.

items.title: Hornets will sign Coby White to a 3-year, $74 million deal, as ESPN first reported. The Pistons, Nets, and others had him on their radar. He gets a chance to start with LaMelo Ball going to the Timberwolves. He passed on a Bulls extension last year and will now earn more per year
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Hornets will sign Coby White to a 3-year, $74 million deal, as ESPN first reported. The Pistons, Nets, and others had him on their radar. He gets a chance to start with LaMelo Ball going to the Timberwolves. He passed on a Bulls extension last year and will now earn more per year.

items.title: This new deal that Coby White and the Hornets agreed to can come in right around his cap hold number. So, Charlotte could still choose to have some cap space this summer, or they can stay over and keep the giant TPE they're creating in the LaMelo Ball trade. Bet on the latter.

items.title: I also feel for Jaden McDaniels.
items.author: Leo S
items.body_excerpt:
I also feel for Jaden McDaniels.

Lost his best friend on the team. Will be asked to play out of position (as currently constructed). If y'all thought Randle was a ball magnet taking touches away from him, wait until you see Jaden playing next to Ant AND LaMelo 😬

items.title: I think the best way to sum it up is this: the Wolves, through their scouting and intel gathering on Ball for years, formed a much different opinion than the public perception of him.  They're betting they're correct. In other words, a zig where others may zag.
items.author: Chris Hine
items.body_excerpt: I think the best way to sum it up is this: the Wolves, through their scouting and intel gathering on Ball for years, formed a much different opinion than the public perception of him.  They're betting they're correct. In other words, a zig where others may zag.

items.title: Everybody thinks they’re “sticking it to the rich guy” when really it’s shooting the city in the foot. There’s a lot of artists who won’t come here because the arena is outdated. And should the Portland Fire also not play in a more up to date arena? Or should we say screw them to

And should the Portland Fire also not play in a more up to date arena? Or should we say screw them too?

items.title: Free agent guard Coby White intends to sign a three-year, $74 million deal to return to the Charlotte Hornets, sources tell ESPN. White was a major priority for the Hornets and now steps into a new starting role in the backcourt.
